questions.text: The energy stored in a charged capacitor is given by
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per fact.

The energy (U) stored in a charged capacitor is given by the formula: U=1/2​CV2Where:C is the capacitance of the capacitor.V is the voltage across the capacitor.So, the correct option will be 1/2 CV². This formula tells us that the energy stored in a capacitor is directly proportional to the square of the voltage across it and the capacitance of the capacitor itself.
Incorrect as per fact.
Incorrect as per fact.

questions.explanation: The energy (U) stored in a charged capacitor is given by the formula: U=1/2​CV2Where:C is the capacitance of the capacitor.V is the voltage across the capacitor.So, the correct option will be 1/2 CV². This formula tells us that the energy stored in a capacitor is directly proportional to the square of the voltage across it and the capacitance of the capacitor itself.

questions.text: The negative of the potential gradient is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per fact and given condition.

The negative of the potential gradient is the Electric Field Intensity.In physics, the electric field is a vector field that shows the direction that a positively charged particle will move when placed in the field. The electric field is defined mathematically as the electric force per unit charge. The potential gradient is a measure of how the electric potential changes with respect to the spatial coordinates. When you take the negative of this, you get the direction of the electric field. This is because the electric field always points from regions of high potential to regions of low potential.
Incorrect as per fact and given condition.
Incorrect as option (b) is correct.

questions.explanation: The negative of the potential gradient is the Electric Field Intensity.In physics, the electric field is a vector field that shows the direction that a positively charged particle will move when placed in the field. The electric field is defined mathematically as the electric force per unit charge. The potential gradient is a measure of how the electric potential changes with respect to the spatial coordinates. When you take the negative of this, you get the direction of the electric field. This is because the electric field always points from regions of high potential to regions of low potential.

questions.text: A wire of uniform cross section "A", Length "L" and resistance "R" is cut in two equal parts. The resistivity of each part is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per calculation and formula: R = ρ L/A
Incorrect as per calculation and formula: R = ρ L/A

The resistivity of a material is a property that is independent of its shape or size. It’s a characteristic of the material itself. So, when you cut a wire into two equal parts, the resistivity of each part remains the same as the original wire.The formula for resistance is given by: R = ρ L/Awhere:R is the resistance,ρ (rho) is the resistivity,L is the length of the wire, andA is the cross-sectional area of the wire.When the wire is cut into two equal parts, the length of each part becomes L/2, but the resistivity (ρ) and the cross-sectional area (A) remain the same. Therefore, the resistivity of each part is still ρ.
Incorrect as per calculation and formula: R = ρ L/A

questions.explanation: The resistivity of a material is a property that is independent of its shape or size. It’s a characteristic of the material itself. So, when you cut a wire into two equal parts, the resistivity of each part remains the same as the original wire.The formula for resistance is given by: R = ρ L/Awhere:R is the resistance,ρ (rho) is the resistivity,L is the length of the wire, andA is the cross-sectional area of the wire.When the wire is cut into two equal parts, the length of each part becomes L/2, but the resistivity (ρ) and the cross-sectional area (A) remain the same. Therefore, the resistivity of each part is still ρ.

questions.text: Joule's law for heat dissipation in Resistance "R" carrying Current "I" is given by:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Joule’s law of electric heating states that the heat dissipated in a resistor is directly proportional to the square of the current passing through it, the resistance it offers to the current, and the time for which the current is passed. It can be mathematically expressed as: W = I2RtWhere:I is the current through the resistor (in amperes),R is the resistance (in ohms), andt is the time for which the current is passed (in seconds).
Incorrect as the correct formula is: W = I2Rt
Incorrect as the correct formula is: W = I2Rt
Incorrect as the correct formula is: W = I2Rt

questions.explanation: Joule’s law of electric heating states that the heat dissipated in a resistor is directly proportional to the square of the current passing through it, the resistance it offers to the current, and the time for which the current is passed. It can be mathematically expressed as: W = I2RtWhere:I is the current through the resistor (in amperes),R is the resistance (in ohms), andt is the time for which the current is passed (in seconds).

questions.text: The resonance frequency of LCR series circuit is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as correct formula is: 1/ 2π√LC
Incorrect as correct formula is: 1/ 2π√LC

The resonance frequency (f) of an LCR series circuit is given by the formula: f = 1/ 2π√LCWhere:L is the inductance of the inductor measured in henries (H),C is the capacitance of the capacitor measured in farads (F),f is the resonance frequency measured in hertz (Hz).This formula gives the frequency at which the circuit will resonate, i.e., the frequency at which the inductive and capacitive reactances are equal, thereby causing the impedance of the circuit to be minimum. At this frequency, the circuit allows maximum current for a given applied voltage. It’s a fundamental concept in the study of AC circuits.
Incorrect as correct formula is: 1/ 2π√LC

questions.explanation: The resonance frequency (f) of an LCR series circuit is given by the formula: f = 1/ 2π√LCWhere:L is the inductance of the inductor measured in henries (H),C is the capacitance of the capacitor measured in farads (F),f is the resonance frequency measured in hertz (Hz).This formula gives the frequency at which the circuit will resonate, i.e., the frequency at which the inductive and capacitive reactances are equal, thereby causing the impedance of the circuit to be minimum. At this frequency, the circuit allows maximum current for a given applied voltage. It’s a fundamental concept in the study of AC circuits.

questions.text: The rest mass of photon is:
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect according to the theory of relativity.

The rest mass of a photon is 0. This is because photons are particles of light and they always move at the speed of light in a vacuum. According to the theory of relativity, any particle that moves at the speed of light must have zero rest mass. Therefore, the rest mass of a photon is 0.m0​=0where (m0) is the rest mass of the photon.
Incorrect according to the theory of relativity.
Incorrect according to the theory of relativity.

According to the theory of relativity, any particle that moves at the speed of light must have zero rest mass. Therefore, the rest mass of a photon is 0.m0​=0where (m0) is the rest mass of the photon.

questions.text: The device that stores electrical energy into magnetic field is called _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as it is a device that stores electrical energy by accumulating electric charges on two closely spaced surfaces that are insulated from each other.
The device that stores electrical energy into a magnetic field is called an inductor. An inductor has the capability to store energy in its magnetic field.
Incorrect as transformer is a passive component that transfers electrical energy from one electrical circuit to another, or multiple circuits.
Incorrect because transformer and capacitor do not store electrical energy into magnetic field.

questions.explanation: The device that stores electrical energy into a magnetic field is called an inductor. An inductor has the capability to store energy in its magnetic field.

questions.text: Shunted Galvanometer is called
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A device that measures multiple electrical properties such as voltage, resistance, and current.

A shunted galvanometer can be referred to as an Ammeter. This is because a shunt, which is a low resistance path, is connected in parallel to the galvanometer to allow it to measure current without being damaged by high currents. The shunt effectively diverts the majority of the current away from the galvanometer’s sensitive coils, allowing it to function as an ammeter.
It measures direct voltage, direct current, and resistance.
An instrument used for measuring electric potential difference between two points in an electric circuit.

questions.explanation: A shunted galvanometer can be referred to as an Ammeter. This is because a shunt, which is a low resistance path, is connected in parallel to the galvanometer to allow it to measure current without being damaged by high currents. The shunt effectively diverts the majority of the current away from the galvanometer’s sensitive coils, allowing it to function as an ammeter.

questions.text: A diode consists of _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: A diode typically consists of one junction. It’s a two-terminal electronic component that conducts current primarily in one direction. The device is made up of a piece of semiconductor material with a P-N junction attached to two electrical terminals. The P-N junction refers to the boundary between the two types of semiconductors, “P” (positive) and “N” (negative). The junction allows current to flow more easily in one direction than the other, which is a fundamental property of a diode.
Incorrect as per fact because diode have only one junction. Two junctions are mostly seen in transistor.
Incorrect as per fact because diode have only one junction. Three junctions are not that much common.
Incorrect as option (a) is correct.

questions.explanation: A diode typically consists of one junction. It’s a two-terminal electronic component that conducts current primarily in one direction. The device is made up of a piece of semiconductor material with a P-N junction attached to two electrical terminals. The P-N junction refers to the boundary between the two types of semiconductors, “P” (positive) and “N” (negative). The junction allows current to flow more easily in one direction than the other, which is a fundamental property of a diode.

questions.text: When a charged particle is projected perpendicular to a uniform snape magnetic field, it's trajectory is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: When a charged particle is projected perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field, its trajectory is a circle. This is due to the Lorentz force which acts as a centripetal force, causing the particle to move in a circular path. The radius of the circle can be determined by the equation: r = mv / qBwhere:m is the mass of the particle,v is the velocity of the particle,q is the charge of the particle, andB is the magnetic field strength.The direction of motion is given by the right-hand rule. If the thumb of the right hand points in the direction of the velocity of the positive charge and the fingers in the direction of the magnetic field, then the force (and hence the acceleration) is in the direction of the palm. For a negative charge, the force is in the opposite direction. This is why the charged particle moves in a circular path.
Incorrect as the trajectory is in circular shape.
Incorrect as the trajectory is in circular shape.
Incorrect as the trajectory is in circular shape.

questions.explanation: When a charged particle is projected perpendicular to a uniform magnetic field, its trajectory is a circle. This is due to the Lorentz force which acts as a centripetal force, causing the particle to move in a circular path. The radius of the circle can be determined by the equation: r = mv / qBwhere:m is the mass of the particle,v is the velocity of the particle,q is the charge of the particle, andB is the magnetic field strength.The direction of motion is given by the right-hand rule. If the thumb of the right hand points in the direction of the velocity of the positive charge and the fingers in the direction of the magnetic field, then the force (and hence the acceleration) is in the direction of the palm. For a negative charge, the force is in the opposite direction. This is why the charged particle moves in a circular path.

questions.text: De- Broglie suggested that every material particle has a wave length associated with it, given by
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as correct formula is: λ = h/mv

According to Louis de Broglie, every material particle has an associated wavelength, which is given by the de Broglie equation: λ = h/mvHere, ( λ ) is the wavelength, ( h ) is Planck’s constant, and ( p=mv) is the momentum of the particle.
Incorrect as correct formula is: λ = h/mv

questions.text: An 'a' particle has the charge _.
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as alpha particle has a charge of '+2e".

An ‘a’ particle, also known as an alpha particle, has a positive charge. Specifically, it has a net electrical charge of +2e. This is because an alpha particle is identical to the nucleus of the helium-4 atom, consisting of two protons and two neutrons bound together. Since protons carry a positive charge, the absence of electrons in an alpha particle results in a net positive charge.
Incorrect as alpha particle has a charge of '+2e".
Incorrect as alpha particle has a charge of '+2e".

This is because an alpha particle is identical to the nucleus of the helium-4 atom, consisting of two protons and two neutrons bound together. Since protons carry a positive charge, the absence of electrons in an alpha particle results in a net positive charge.

Incorrect as per law.

questions.text: Mathematical form of the uncertainty principle is
questions.subject: Physics

options.explanation: Incorrect as per Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle.
Incorrect as per Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle.

This equation: Δx × Δp = h / 4πis known as the Heisenberg Uncertainty Principle. It’s a fundamental concept in quantum mechanics that states it is impossible to simultaneously measure the exact position. The more precisely one of these values is known, the less precisely the other can be known.
